messages.content: When do new students have to finish course selection in SIS?
For the 2026-2027 course catalog, new students must complete course selection in SIS by May 1. [source: course catalog]
Did Webb replace AP courses with something else?
Yes. The catalog says Webb discontinued AP courses in fall 2025 and replaced them with Advanced Studies courses. [source: course catalog]
Does Webb host AP exams on campus?
No. Webb says it is not a testing center for AP exams and does not administer them on campus. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
How does Webb describe its humanities graduation requirement?

Webb says the usual separate English and history requirement is replaced by a Humanities requirement equivalent to seven full-year courses. Humanities electives can satisfy either English or history requirements. [source: course catalog]
Can a student get team sport credit by managing a team?
Yes, in some cases. The catalog says juniors or seniors can earn team sport credit as a manager for certain programs marked for that purpose. [source: course catalog]
Does Sports Broadcasting count for team sport credit?
For juniors or seniors, yes. The catalog says they can earn team sport credit through Sports Broadcasting. [source: course catalog]
What season is Debate offered in the afternoon program?
Debate is offered in the fall. The catalog describes it as an introduction to the Webb debate program. [source: course catalog]
What is Paleontology Museum Lab actually like?

It is hands-on museum work. The catalog says students may help clean fossils in the prep lab, laser scan dinosaur bones, and maintain exhibits, and no prior experience is required. [source: course catalog]
Is Science Fair Research Lab just for experienced researchers?

Not necessarily. Webb describes it as a place where students explore independent scientific research with support and mentorship in research design, data generation, and public presentation. [source: course catalog]
What does Music Performance include?

It is a winter afternoon activity open to instrumentalists, vocalists, and pianists. Depending on the group, students may do coached practice, small ensembles, jazz combo work, individualized study, or a self-designed music project. [source: course catalog]
When does the fall theater production happen?
The catalog says the fall theater program rehearses and produces a full-length play for a three-night run in November. [source: course catalog]
What is Musical Theater at Webb?

It is a spring afternoon program centered on rehearsing and producing a full-length musical for a three-night run in May. The work includes vocal technique, movement, script analysis, and song interpretation. [source: course catalog]
Does Webb say anything specific about weekend trips?
Yes. Webb says it organizes weekend trips to Los Angeles and Claremont Village and that students can sign up through the Student News group on Outlook. [source: after school and weekends]
What is one thing Webb emphasizes about living in Southern California?
Webb presents Southern California as an extension of the campus, giving students access to major cultural, academic, artistic, and industry opportunities. [source: about]
Does Webb say anything about student publications besides just listing them?
Yes. The handbook says student publications are part of Webb's educational program and are meant to help students engage in voice and expression responsibly. [source: handbook]
Who is the Dean of Enrollment Management?
Jamila Everett is listed as Dean of Enrollment Management. [source: faculty]
Who leads athletics and afternoon activities?
Stephen Wishek is listed as Director of Athletics & Afternoon Activities. [source: directory]
What does Webb say about dorm life teaching students?
Webb says dorm life teaches skills like personal responsibility, self-advocacy, time management, teamwork, resilience, and effective communication. [source: dorm life]
Does Webb have a non-discrimination policy in admissions?
Yes. The admissions page says Webb admits students without discrimination on protected grounds and applies that standard to its programs and policies. [source: admissions]
What does the chapel program ask seniors to do?
Seniors are invited to craft and deliver Chapel Talks reflecting on what they have learned during their time at Webb. [source: chapel program]
How does Webb describe the role of the College Guidance Office?
Webb says the office works with each student and family to identify a range of colleges that fit the student's continued academic success. [source: college guidance]
What percentage of Webb graduates go to selective four-year colleges?

The college profile says 99 percent of graduates gain admission to selective four-year colleges and universities, while 1 percent choose an alternative path such as a gap year. [source: college guidance profile]
Does Webb publish National Merit numbers?

Yes. Its 2024-25 college profile lists five-year totals including 14 winners, 40 finalists, 46 semifinalists, 99 letters of commendation, and 29 National Hispanic Scholars. [source: college guidance profile]
What does Webb say about service in the local community?
Webb highlights student service in the Pomona Valley and describes recent work helping distribute groceries to more than 250 community members. [source: community impact]
Does Webb list Artificial Intelligence Club?
Yes. Artificial Intelligence Club appears on the 2025 clubs list. [source: clubs and affinity groups]
How should I think about choosing between Art AB and Filmmaking Studio I?

Choose based on the kind of making you want to do. Art AB is the better starting point if you want broad visual-art foundations with materials and hands-on studio work. Filmmaking Studio I is better if you want camera-based storytelling, editing, and short-film production.
What is a smart way to decide between Hon French 3 and French 4 later on?

Think about pace, confidence, and the rest of your schedule. The harder path is only better if you have strong language foundations and enough room to sustain the workload without dragging down everything else.
How would you explain the difference between a prerequisite and department approval?

A prerequisite is the formal course background listed in the catalog. Department approval means the department still has to judge whether the student is actually ready, even if the prerequisite is technically met.
What is the best mindset for building a balanced schedule?

Treat balance as a performance strategy, not as a sign of weakness. A strong schedule should stretch you, but it should still leave room to do meaningful work, keep commitments, and stay steady through the year.
How should someone compare AdvSt Statistics and Calculus if they are not sure what they want to study?

Calculus keeps a more traditional math progression open. AdvSt Statistics is often stronger for students drawn to data, research, social science, business, or applied analysis. If you are unsure, think about which kind of thinking you want more of next year: abstract mathematical structure or evidence-based interpretation.
What does it mean when Webb says a schedule should show an incremental increase in difficulty?

It means rigor should usually build over time in a controlled way instead of jumping wildly all at once. Colleges often read that as maturity: the student challenged themselves more as they grew, but did not make reckless choices.
How should a student choose between Robotics and Science Fair Research Lab?

Robotics is better if you want team-based engineering, design, programming, and competition. Science Fair Research Lab is better if you want independent scientific inquiry, mentorship, and research presentation. One is collaborative engineering; the other is more individual research.
If a student likes both writing and politics, how should they think about humanities electives?

Start with the actual kind of work you enjoy most. Some humanities electives lean more toward literature and writing, while others lean more toward ideas, history, culture, or public argument. Pick the courses whose day-to-day thinking feels most alive to you, not just the ones with the grandest-sounding titles.
What is the right way to think about a workload 1.25 course?

Treat it as a signal that the course is expected to demand more sustained time and intensity than a workload 1 course. It is not just a label. It should affect how many other demanding things you stack around it.
How do I decide whether to continue in an art sequence or switch into something new?

Ask which choice gives you more real growth. Continuing makes sense if you want depth, stronger technique, or a more serious portfolio. Switching makes sense if your interest has moved and the new course opens a kind of work you genuinely want to explore.
What is a good way to judge whether an outside program away from Webb is worth proposing?

It should do something Webb cannot already do for you in the same way. A strong proposal usually has a clear academic reason, good course compatibility, and a convincing explanation of why it is worth leaving the normal Webb program for a semester or year.
How should someone compare Theater and Technical Theater in the afternoon program?

Theater is the better fit if you want performance, acting, and live character work. Technical Theater is the better fit if you are more interested in design, sets, sound, lighting, production systems, and backstage problem-solving. Both are serious, but they reward different instincts.
How should a student think about Chinese 4 versus AdvSt Chinese: Language and Culture?

Chinese 4 is the better choice if you want strong continuation without automatically pushing to the most advanced option. AdvSt Chinese makes more sense if you already have the foundation and want a deeper, faster, more demanding capstone-style language experience.
What does a student gain by going to office hours before there is a crisis?
They catch confusion while it is still small. Early help turns a class into something manageable instead of letting small gaps pile up into bigger ones that feel harder to admit and harder to fix.
How should a student choose between Neuroscience and AdvSt Anatomy & Physiology?

Neuroscience is often the better entry if you are more curious about the brain, behavior, and cognitive systems. AdvSt Anatomy & Physiology is stronger if you want a more body-systems-oriented, advanced biological course with heavier structure and depth. Choose based on what questions you want to spend the year answering.
What is a smart way to decide between another honors course and protecting time for leadership?

Ask which one adds more substance to your year. Another honors course only helps if it fits well and you can do it strongly. Leadership can be the better investment if it is real, time-intensive, and central to how you contribute to your community.
How do you tell whether a class is challenging in a good way or just wrong for you?
A healthy challenge usually leads to growth when you get help and stay consistent. A bad fit keeps feeling unstable even with real effort, and it starts taking too much from the rest of your life.
What is the practical difference between getting by in a class and actually being well placed?

Getting by means you may be surviving the class. Being well placed means the class is difficult but sustainable, and you can still learn deeply, ask good questions, and keep the rest of your schedule functioning.
How should someone compare HMC/Webb CS with Technology and Innovation Seminar II if they already know they like tech?

HMC/Webb CS is the clearer choice if you want a direct advanced computer science signal. Technology and Innovation Seminar II can be better if you want broader applied tech work and innovation-oriented thinking without making the year only about formal CS.
What is this year's Jubilee date?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Event dates like that should be checked in the current school calendar or official announcements.
Who is my advisor going to be next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Advisor assignments are student-specific and would need to come from the school.
Did my teacher already approve my planned absence form?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The safest source would be the actual approval communication or the office handling the form.
Are there still open spots in Honors Chamber Singers?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Current enrollment and space are live scheduling details, so I would not want to guess.
What room is Debate meeting in after school today?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That is a current logistical detail that should come from the latest student communication.
Did I get selected for Admission Fellows?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. That would need to come from the leadership selection notice or the relevant office.
Is there definitely an overnight Unbounded Days trip option next cycle?

I can't confirm that from the information I have. Webb says Unbounded Days offerings are released in the program catalogue, so the exact mix should be checked there when it comes out. [source: handbook]
Will the same dorm head still be in my dorm next year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Housing staff assignments can change, so I would check the current residential information when it is released.
What is the exact acceptance rate right now?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. I would not want to guess at a current admissions statistic without an official source.
Did my AP testing request get approved?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Since Webb treats those requests as rare and case-specific, the answer would need to come from the relevant school communication. [source: handbook]
Is Prom off campus this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Event location is the kind of detail that should be checked through current student announcements.
What are the tryout dates for girls golf next season?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The athletics materials show team information and results, but tryout dates are current-season logistics.
Which junior got the strongest college results this year?
I can't confirm that from the information I have, and I would not want to invent or rank private student outcomes.
Is there a spot left in Alamo right now?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. Current room availability would need to come from Residential Life.
Will there be a science fair trip this winter for sure?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The catalog confirms Science Fair Research Lab exists, but not every associated trip or current-year plan. [source: course catalog]
Can you tell me if this is okay?
What are you trying to check: a course choice, a policy issue, an email, or something else?
What do I do now?
What happened, and is this about academics, a school rule, a schedule decision, or something personal?
Can you make it sound better?
Yes. Paste the sentence or draft, and tell me whether you want it to sound warmer, more formal, shorter, or more direct.
Who do I email?
About what issue?
What is the rule on this?
Which rule do you mean: attendance, tutoring, course changes, housing, or something else?
Can you compare the classes?
Yes. Which two classes do you want compared?
I need a school message.
Who is it going to, and what do you need the message to ask or explain?
Does this count as enough?
Enough for what exactly: graduation, college rigor, a requirement, or something else?
How bad is this?
What are you referring to: a grade, a missed deadline, a conduct issue, or a schedule problem?
What are my options?
For which situation?
Can I appeal it?
What decision are you trying to appeal?
I need help planning next year.
What grade will you be in, and are you trying to optimize for rigor, balance, a certain field, or something else?
What support should I use?
Do you mean academic help, tutoring, counseling, accommodations, or something different?
Can you explain the sequence?
Which department or course sequence are you asking about?
Hey, can you help me phrase something to a teacher?
Yes. Paste what you want to say, and I will help shape it.
Thanks for explaining that.
Glad it helped.
Write a fake note saying I was sick.
I can't help write a false note. If you want, I can help you write an honest message explaining what happened and asking what the make-up process is.
Can you make this sentence less awkward: I was wanting to ask if maybe you had time?
A cleaner version is: I wanted to ask whether you might have time.
I am overwhelmed and feel like I am behind in everything.

Shrink the problem first. List what is actually due, circle the two things with the nearest consequences, and do the easier of those two first. Momentum matters more than trying to solve your whole life in one burst.
How do I sound respectful without sounding overly formal?
Be direct, use a normal greeting, and say clearly what you need. Respect usually sounds calm and specific, not stiff.
What is a one-sentence difference between fair and equal?
Equal means everyone gets the same thing, while fair means people get what they reasonably need for a just outcome.
I need a short apology text for being late to a meeting.
Sorry I was late today. That was on me, and I appreciate your patience.
Can you make this follow-up less sharp: I already emailed and still have no answer.
You could say: I wanted to follow up on my earlier email in case it got buried. When you have a chance, I would appreciate an update. Thank you.
How do I ask for help without sounding dramatic?
State the problem early and specifically. For example: I am starting to struggle with this material, and I want to address it before it gets worse.
Can you turn these notes into a cleaner paragraph?
Yes. Send the notes, and I will shape them into a clear paragraph.
I got one bad result and now I feel like everything is collapsing.

One bad result can feel huge in the moment, but it does not automatically define the whole pattern. The useful move is to figure out what this one result actually changes, then respond to that rather than to the panic around it.
What is a simple way to say I need more time to think?
You can say: I would like a little more time to think this through carefully.
Can you help me decline something politely?
Yes. Tell me what you are declining and how formal you want it to sound.
How do I make an email sound more confident?
Cut hedging. Replace phrases like maybe, just, or if possible unless they are truly needed. Clear purpose and calm wording usually sound more confident than extra politeness.
I want the hardest-looking schedule possible, but I also want to keep my grades high and stay sane.
Those goals can pull against each other. A schedule only looks strong if you can actually perform in it.
So you would not just max out everything?
No. I would choose the strongest schedule that still lets you do real work well, not the one that looks impressive in isolation.
I got recommended into three advanced classes, and now I feel guilty even considering taking only two.

Recommendation means you are seen as capable, not that you are obligated to say yes to all of them. The better question is which combination gives you the best year, not which combination proves the most.
So turning one down is not automatically wasting an opportunity?
Right. Sometimes it is the smarter use of the opportunity because it protects your ability to do the rest well.
Use only confirmed Webb policy facts when answering school-rule questions. If something is not confirmed, say so clearly.
Can students order food to campus on a random Wednesday night?
No during the academic week. The handbook says students may not order or receive food deliveries during the academic week. [source: handbook]
I want to ask about switching out of a class, but I do not want the message to sound impulsive or emotional.

Keep the focus on fit and judgment rather than frustration. You can say that you have been trying to assess the course carefully and would appreciate guidance on whether a change is worth considering.
Can you draft that?

Hi [Name], I wanted to ask whether it might make sense to discuss my placement in [course]. I have been trying to assess the fit carefully, and I would appreciate your guidance on whether a change is worth considering. Thank you. Best, [Your Name]
I care about college apps, but I also know I do my best work when my life is balanced. Those feel like opposite philosophies.
They only look opposite if you assume colleges value exhaustion more than substance. In reality, balance often makes the work stronger.
So balance is not a soft option?
Not at all. It can be the disciplined option when it leads to better work, steadier involvement, and more believable choices.
Do not validate rumors. If the materials do not confirm it, say so plainly.
People are saying AP exams might quietly come back. Is that true?

I can't confirm that from the information I have. The current materials say Webb discontinued AP courses and does not offer AP exams on campus, so I would wait for an official update before assuming anything changed. [source: handbook] [source: course catalog]
I need to write a message to my group partner who keeps disappearing, but I do not want to sound explosive.
The goal is to sound firm, not furious. Name the problem, what is still missing, and the next deadline.
Can you write it?

Hey, I need to be direct that communication has dropped off, and it is affecting the project. We still need [specific pieces] by [time]. If you cannot do your part, please tell me as soon as possible so we can make a realistic plan.
When uncertain about live logistics, avoid guessing and point to the current official source.
What time is the next girls tennis match exactly?
I can't confirm that from the information I have. The athletics page shows results and general team information, but exact upcoming match times should be checked on the current team schedule.
I feel stuck between taking the higher level because I can maybe do it and taking the lower one because I think I would actually learn more.
That is a real decision, and 'I can maybe do it' is not always strong enough by itself. The better question is where you will do the most meaningful work, not where you can barely hang on.
So there are cases where the lower level is the stronger choice?
Yes. If it leads to deeper learning, stronger performance, and a healthier overall year, it can absolutely be the stronger choice.
Can you help me ask for outside tutoring without making it sound like I am trying to go around the teacher?
Yes. The key is to mention that you are continuing to use school support and are asking whether additional support would make sense, not demanding a workaround.
Draft it for me.

Hi [Name], I wanted to ask about the process for considering outside tutoring in [subject]. I am continuing to use school support, including [office hours/teacher help], but I wanted to ask whether additional support might be appropriate and, if so, what the next steps would be. Best, [Your Name]
I want to sound mature in a message, but every time I try, it sounds robotic.
That usually happens when the writing is trying too hard to sound formal. Mature writing is usually just clear, calm, and specific.
What should I cut first?
Cut inflated phrases and repeated apologies. Keep one greeting, one clear purpose, and one respectful close.
Keep answers grounded and concise when the user asks about official contacts or procedures.
Who handles academic questions broadly?
The handbook lists Nina Butler-Roberts for Academics. [source: handbook]
What is the difference between a school requirement and a college recommendation?
A school requirement is what you need to graduate. A college recommendation is what may be suggested if you want a stronger academic profile for more selective admissions.
Can you compare Pomona Valley service work and peer mentoring as forms of contribution?

Service work usually shows outward community engagement and willingness to help beyond your immediate circle. Peer mentoring shows sustained investment in people within your school community. Neither is automatically better. They just tell different stories about how you contribute.
What does it mean that Webb is interdisciplinary?

It means Webb tries to connect subjects rather than treating them as sealed-off boxes. You can see that in things like its integrated humanities model, experiential learning emphasis, and the way it links classroom work to real-world settings in Southern California. [source: about] [source: course catalog]
How do I know whether I am choosing a class because I actually want it or because I want the label?

A useful test is to imagine nobody else ever seeing the course title. If you would still want the work, the reading, and the daily thinking, it is probably real interest. If most of the appeal disappears without the audience, the label may be doing more of the work than you think.
What is one practical benefit of Webb being so close to the Claremont Colleges?
It gives students easier access to nearby academic and cultural resources, which Webb repeatedly presents as part of its educational environment. [source: about] [source: after school and weekends]
How should I think about whether an extracurricular is worth keeping if my schedule gets tougher?
Keep the activities that either matter deeply to you or contribute strongly to your growth. When time tightens, the weakest commitments are usually the ones you only keep out of habit or fear.
What does it mean when a school says it values character in athletics?

It usually means athletics is not only about performance or winning. At Webb, the descriptions often tie sports to sportsmanship, integrity, teamwork, and mutual support, which suggests the school treats athletics as part of personal development too. [source: course catalog]
How do I ask a teacher if I am in the wrong level without sounding like I am giving up?
Frame it as a fit question, not a surrender. For example: I wanted your honest view on whether this level still looks like the right fit for me within my overall schedule.
Can you rewrite this to sound less panicked: I am kind of freaking out because I think I may have chosen wrong.
A calmer version is: I am concerned that I may not have made the best choice, and I would appreciate your perspective on it.
What is a good way to say no to a leadership opportunity if I am already overloaded?
You can say: Thank you for thinking of me. I really appreciate the opportunity, but I would not be able to give the role the time and energy it deserves, so I need to decline.
I need to tell someone I changed my mind, but I do not want to sound flaky.
Try: After thinking it through more carefully, I realized a different option makes more sense for me. That sounds deliberate rather than unreliable.
